title: Bluff: Science Myths
description: One answer is fake — can you spot the bluff?

questions.question: What is the hardest natural substance on Earth?
questions.explanation: Diamond scores 10 on the Mohs hardness scale, the highest possible.

questions.question: What is the most abundant gas in Earth's atmosphere?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen makes up about 78% of Earth's atmosphere.

questions.question: What is the speed of light in km/s?
questions.explanation: Light travels at approximately 299,792 km/s in a vacuum.

questions.question: Which planet has the most moons?
questions.explanation: Saturn has over 140 confirmed moons, surpassing Jupiter's count.

questions.question: What temperature is absolute zero in Celsius?
questions.explanation: Absolute zero is -273.15°C, where all molecular motion stops.

questions.question: What is the most common element in the universe?
questions.explanation: Hydrogen makes up about 75% of all normal matter in the universe.

questions.question: How many bones does an adult human have?
questions.explanation: Adults have 206 bones. Babies are born with about 270, which fuse over time.

questions.question: What is the largest organ in the human body?
questions.explanation: The skin is the largest organ, covering about 2 square meters in adults.

questions.question: Which blood type is the universal donor?
questions.explanation: O-negative can be given to anyone regardless of their blood type.

questions.question: What is the chemical formula for table salt?
How many chromosomes do humans have?
Which metal is liquid at room temperature?
questions.explanation: Mercury is the only metal that is liquid at standard room temperature.

questions.question: What is the closest star to Earth (besides the Sun)?
questions.explanation: Proxima Centauri is about 4.24 light-years from Earth.

questions.question: What percentage of the human body is water?
questions.explanation: The adult human body is approximately 60% water.

questions.question: What is the pH of pure water?
questions.explanation: Pure water has a neutral pH of exactly 7.
